note: Combination of early Naskh and Maghribi scripts | Same script as UTO Homily on the Rich and the Poor. Large bowls with sweeping curves for a number of descending strokes. Occasionally the stems of letter alif have a club-like extension to the left of their top point, and the loop of letter Ḍād and letter ṣād has no “tooth”. The base stroke of the initial letter ‘ayn is elongated backward to the right. Sporadic diacritical marks

bib.citation: Shoemaker, Stephen J. Ancient Traditions of the Virgin Mary’s Dormition and Assumption. Oxford: University Press, 2002
Graf, Georg. Geschichte der Christlichen Arabischen Literatur. Vol. 1. Vatican City, 1944.
Enger, Maximilian. Akhbär Yühanna as-salîh fi-naqlat umm al-Masih, id est Joannis Apostoli de transitu Beatae Mariae Virginis Liber. Eberfeld: R. L. Friderichs, 1854

program.label: Sinai Palimpsests Project
program.description: Imaged and studied as part of the Sinai Palimpsests Project (2011–2016), a collaboration between St. Catherine’s Monastery of the Sinai, the Early Manuscripts Electronic Library, and UCLA Library, funded by the Arcadia Fund and the Ahmanson Foundation. The project used multispectral imaging and scholarly analysis to recover and document erased and obscured texts in palimpsest manuscripts from the monastery’s collection.
